summary.intent: Merge the main branch into feat/voice-base-url and resolve any merge conflicts that arose.
summary.outcome: Conflicts in whisper.rs and voice.rs were resolved by combining both branches' additions, all 17 whisper tests pass, and the merge commit plus a follow-up Cargo.lock commit were created successfully.

repo: The project is a Rust workspace (molt/moltis) with at least two relevant crates: crates/gateway and crates/voice.
WhisperStt uses a builder-style API with with_options() and a with_model() convenience method.
The voice provider supports keyless local servers via an is_configured() check, allowing operation without an API key when base_url points to a local instance.
Some files contain === sequences (likely in comments) that can produce false positives when grepping for conflict markers.

code.finding: with_options() now accepts 4 params: (api_key, base_url, model, language). with_model() is retained as a convenience method sourced from main. Both branches' tests are included.
Whisper provider construction passes base_url (from feat branch) and language (from main), and uses is_configured() to support keyless local servers.

workflow: SSH authentication via YubiKey can block git fetch operations; a retry after tapping the key resolves it.
Running targeted crate-level tests (whisper-related) after a merge provides confidence before finalizing the merge commit.
Cargo.lock changes after merging main with new dependencies should be committed separately as a clean-up step.

friction: YubiKey SSH authentication required a manual tap mid-session, blocking the initial git fetch and requiring a retry.
False-positive conflict marker matches (=== in comment separators in other files) required extra verification that no real markers remained.
